vocab.meaning: tâm lý bầy đàn (thích làm theo đám đông)
vocab.example: As a child, I never really succumbed to herd mentality; I was perfectly content playing by myself in the garden.

vocab.meaning: sự suy ngẫm/trầm ngâm một mình
vocab.example: I often gravitated towards solitary contemplation, finding that drawing alone allowed me to focus better than in a noisy group.
I thrived in a collaborative spirit, especially when my friends and I worked together to build elaborate sandcastles.

vocab.meaning: người năng nổ giao thiệp/thích tiệc tùng, đám đông
vocab.example: I wasn't exactly a social butterfly, but I did enjoy the occasional team sport to burn off some energy.

vocab.meaning: sự tương tác với bạn bè đồng trang lứa
vocab.example: My parents encouraged peer interaction because they felt it was vital for developing my communication skills.

vocab.meaning: xu hướng khép kín/thích ở một mình
vocab.example: I had somewhat reclusive tendencies during my early years, often burying my nose in a book for hours.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực/hoạt động chung của tập thể
vocab.example: There was a unique thrill in a collective endeavor, like when we organized a neighborhood treasure hunt.
Playing alone gave me a great sense of autonomy, as I could make all the rules of my imaginary world.
Due to my extroverted inclination, I always felt a bit restless if I didn't have a playmate to share activities with.

vocab.meaning: các mối quan hệ/động lực tương tác giữa người với người
vocab.example: Group activities were fascinating because I loved observing the interpersonal dynamics and leadership roles among my peers.
